methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Hidden Level is a Series C defense and security company founded by Jeff Cole and Gary Dominicos, currently valued at $500M post-money after raising $130M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 2 public and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, projects a value of $850M from the $500M last post-money valuation. Peer-median analysis across 9 defense and security companies at comparable or adjacent stages yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $9.2B. The sample includes Series D firms Saronic ($9.2B) and Chaos Industries ($4.5B), as well as Series B peers Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI, all valued at $1.4B–$1.5B.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $722M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $1.1B. This range reflects the wide dispersion within the peer set, driven primarily by stage heterogeneity and market positioning variance. The current $500M valuation sits below both method implications, suggesting room for upside in a Series D context. Key uncertainties include Hidden Level's specific revenue multiples relative to peers and customer concentration risk within the defense sector.
